page: Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g is an accelerator-optimized server configured with 16 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs providing a total of 640 GB of VRAM. The host system is powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores operating at 2.2 GHz, supported by 1360.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides 14.17 GB of RAM per core but does not include any local NVMe storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a significant divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the bottom 25% tier. Conversely, multi-core and parallelized execution are highly efficient, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU score, and PassMark CPU extended instructions testing in the top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally strong, reaching 4205.12 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 193.62 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Redis SET operations also demonstrate high throughput at 61.66 million operations per second.

Given the massive GPU and memory footprint, this server represents a highly specialized hardware profile where cost efficiency is realized through parallel processing density rather than general-purpose compute. The lack of local storage requires external storage integration, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This server is optimized for highly parallelized workloads, including large-scale deep learning training, massive GPU-accelerated simulations, high-performance computing, and memory-intensive parallel data processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for highly parallelized workloads. It features 16 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 640 GB of total VRAM, paired with an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor offering 96 vCPUs, 48 physical cores, and 1360.0 GB of system memory. While single-core CPU benchmarks and single-threaded compression tasks perform in the bottom 25%, the server excels in multi-core execution and memory bandwidth, with stress-ng multi-core performance and uncached read bandwidth scoring in the top 25%. Redis SET operations reach 61.66 million operations per second. The server lacks local storage, requiring external storage attachments. It is best suited for GPU-accelerated deep learning, high-performance computing, and large-scale parallel data processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 16 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 640 GB VRAM, 96 vCPUs, and 1360 GB RAM, designed for parallel computing and deep learning.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g features 16 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs, 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 1360 GB of RAM for highly parallelized GPU workloads.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized platform combining sixteen Ampere GPUs and massive system memory for highly parallelized high-performance computing workloads.
bullet_points: Equipped with 16 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs delivering 640 GB of total VRAM
Features 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1360.0 GB of system memory
Delivers strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth
Lacks local storage, requiring external storage configurations
Optimized for deep learning training and parallelized scientific simulations
